entries.title: Students read about the story of cartography from ancient Mesopotamia up through the discovery of Antarctica in the late 1800s and draw maps along the way. The course finishes with a term of historical geography where students follow the steps of Canadian explorers from the 19th century and learn about Canadian provinces through map work.
entries.label: Students read about the story of cartography from ancient Mesopotamia up through the discovery of Antarctica in the late 1800s and draw maps along the way. The course finishes with a term of historical geography where students follow the steps of Canadian explorers from the 19th century and learn about Canadian provinces through map work.
entries.content: The first two terms are the same every year and shared between both US and Canadian courses. Term 3 brings in resources from the historical time period and is specific to Canadian students.
